title: nautilocalyx bracteatus
intro: Nautilocalyx bracteatus, also known as the 'Dwarf Nautilocalyx', is a flowering plant species within the family Boraginaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central America and the Caribbean. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Nautilocalyx bracteatus include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be used to make teas or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
